name: Vantage
description: Cloud cost visibility and FinOps platform that helps engineering, finance, and FinOps teams track, attribute and optimize infrastructure spend across AWS, Azure, GCP and other providers.

details.targetMarket: Engineering teams, FinOps and finance teams at startups, scaleups and mid-market enterprises managing multi-cloud infrastructure

items: Unified multi-cloud cost dashboards (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Kubernetes cost reporting and unit costs
Vantage Autopilot for AWS Savings Plans (automated commitment purchases)
Automated FinOps Agent for remediation and chat-driven workflows (AI-enabled)
Native integrations: AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, Datadog, Snowflake, Databricks, MongoDB, Fastly, Vercel, and more
Developer API and Terraform provider (FinOps as Code)
SOC 1 and SOC 2 compliance (listed on pricing/features page)
Audit logs and enterprise support options

missingFeatures: Full automated optimization across non-AWS commitments (e.g., RIs/Savings Plans equivalents on other clouds)
Deeper Kubernetes/container efficiency automation (hands-off rightsizing and autoscaling-driven remediation)
More advanced, fully-customizable dashboarding and chart types (customers report limited chart options)
Simpler pricing tiers for rapidly growing teams (tier jumps based on tracked spend can be steep)
Broader out-of-the-box SaaS & PaaS coverage for less common providers (some integrations are still on the roadmap)

pricingPlans.name: Starter
pricingPlans.note: Free tier for up to $2,500 of tracked cloud spend, 3 users, 6 months data retention, basic support and 27 integrations.

pricingPlans.name: Pro
pricingPlans.note: Self-service tier for small startups: up to $7,500 tracked cloud spend, Autopilot for AWS Savings Plans, virtual tagging, 5 users.

pricingPlans.name: Business
pricingPlans.note: For growing companies: up to $20,000 tracked spend, extended data retention, more users and same-day support.

pricingPlans.name: Enterprise
pricingPlans.note: Custom pricing for unlimited tracked spend, dedicated account rep, Automated FinOps Agent, enterprise SLAs and integrations.

strengths: Fast time-to-value and straightforward setup for multi-account/cloud environments
Powerful cost allocation via virtual tagging and granular segmentation
Modern UX plus automation options (Autopilot for Savings Plans and AI-powered FinOps Agent)
weaknesses: Automation focus is strongest on AWS Savings Plans — fewer automated commitment flows for other clouds
Optimization automation and agent features incur separate fees (percentage of savings or token charges), which can complicate ROI calculations
Dashboarding and chart customization are reported as limited by some customers
